style.input: I'm deciding between LoRA and full fine-tuning for a 7B model on a single GPU. How should I choose?
style.note: 开放性技术选择题，看回答是探索式还是直接给答案

style.input: How would you approach building a personal AI system that learns from your own conversations?
style.note: 项目核心话题，看是否展示迭代思维而非单一结论

style.input: What's the best way to structure training data for fine-tuning?
style.note: 有多种有效答案，看是否探索而非直接断言

style.input: I want to make my AI agent remember important conversations. Where do I start?
style.note: 开放设计问题，优质回答应拆解假设再给建议

style.input: Should I use vector search or keyword search for my agent's memory system?
style.note: 技术权衡题，好的回答应该先问具体需求再给建议

style.input: My AI agent keeps giving generic answers. How do I make it more personalized?
style.note: 诊断式问题，应展示排查思路而非列举方案

style.input: Is it better to have many small plugins or one large agent for a personal AI system?
style.note: 架构权衡，应体现情境依赖性思维

style.input: How do you evaluate whether a fine-tuned model is actually better?
style.note: 元认知问题，应质疑'更好'的定义而非直接给指标

style.input: What does it mean for an AI to understand context?
style.note: 概念性问题，好答案应先解构概念再讨论

style.input: I have 1000 conversations. How should I turn them into training data?
style.note: 核心pipeline问题，看是否主动问数据质量/类型

context.content: I think user-to-assistant turn segmentation is the right way to structure training data.
Yes, that's the standard approach. Each user message paired with the assistant reply creates a clean training sample.

trajectory.input: But I noticed some of my conversations don't look like Q&A at all. They iterate.
trajectory.note: 应能识别这是修正信号，引导向认知图谱方向

context.content: My fine-tuned model seems to answer faster but the responses feel shallow.
That could be due to overfitting on short, direct answer patterns.

trajectory.input: Wait, actually I think the issue might be something about how I structured the training prompts.
trajectory.note: 用户自我修正，模型应跟上而不是坚持之前的诊断

context.content: I want my agent to remember everything.
You can store all conversations in a vector database and retrieve them with semantic search.

trajectory.input: Actually, I realized not everything should be remembered. Some things should expire.
trajectory.note: 目标修正，应放弃之前的建议并重新思考

context.content: I think timestamps in training data are just metadata, not useful for training.
Timestamps can be useful for filtering or sorting data during preprocessing.

trajectory.input: Hmm, but what if the time gap between two similar statements actually means the person changed their mind?
trajectory.note: 用户提出新框架，应能认识到这改变了整个问题的性质

context.content: My agent is too agreeable. It just validates everything I say.
You might want to add instructions in the system prompt to push back more.

trajectory.input: I tried that but it feels forced. The pushback doesn't feel natural.
trajectory.note: 第一个方案失败，应放弃并探索根本原因

correction.input: Fine-tuning just means teaching the model new facts it didn't know before.
correction.note: 常见误解，应温和纠正：fine-tuning改的是行为模式而非知识

correction.input: If I give the model more examples of good answers, it will automatically learn to reason better.
correction.note: 混淆了输出质量与推理能力，应澄清这个区别

correction.input: A model with better perplexity score on my dataset is definitely a better model for my use case.
correction.note: 混淆了指标与目标，应指出perplexity的局限性

correction.input: The more training data I have, the better my fine-tuned model will be.
correction.note: 数量≠质量的误区，应引入数据质量和分布的概念

correction.input: My personal AI should learn everything about me and never forget anything.
correction.note: 完美记忆的幻觉，应引入选择性记忆和遗忘的价值

correction.input: Since my fine-tuned model is trained on my conversations, it will think like me.
correction.note: 混淆了行为模仿与认知结构，应区分这两个层次

correction.input: I can just use ChatGPT outputs as training data for my model since they're high quality.
correction.note: 版权/许可问题 + 分布问题，应同时指出两个层面

correction.input: Vector similarity search always finds the most relevant memory.
correction.note: 语义相似≠相关，应指出向量搜索的局限

correction.input: If my model was trained last year, I need to retrain it completely to update it.
correction.note: 全量重训不是唯一选项，应介绍增量/持续学习

correction.input: The best way to evaluate my personal AI is to measure how accurately it answers factual questions.
correction.note: 评估目标错配，个人AI的核心价值不是事实准确性
